title: Browser Trust, CORS, CSRF, Cookies, and Security Headers · Type-System Trace: Cors Origin Method Fields
summary: A browser API reflects origins, combines credentials with wildcard sharing, treats CORS as access control, relies only on SameSite, and emits weak cookie and framing policy. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsts cors origin method fields increment in browser request and response-sharing policy matrix. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Implement an exact origin allowlist, preflight method and field checks, credentials, exposed fields, Vary, cache duration, null origin policy, and failure behavior.
Choose 401, 403, or concealed 404 behavior consistently and preserve bounded decision evidence without leaking sensitive resource existence or policy internals.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Node and Express server Predict Cors Origin Method Fields · Theory Cors Origin Method Fields
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory team handles case 8422 in a document service receiving slow compressed uploads. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to keep CORS response sharing distinct from CSRF mutation authorization; record only bounded redacted diagnostic fields; then fail the canary while the old revision drains and prove rollback remains safe. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Implement an exact origin allowlist, preflight method and field checks, credentials, exposed fields, Vary, cache duration, null origin policy, and failure behavior.
